content.text: The system shall provide an "Add New Observation " button in the header of the currently selected Activity Phase.
an "Add New Observation Logged" button in the Activity Phase header
I can add new observations for that phase.
I am viewing an Activity Phase,
the header displays a button “Add New Observation Logged.”
the Add Observation side panel must open.
When the user clicks "Add New Observation Logged", the system shall open a side panel (drawer) titled "Add New Observation".
a dedicated side panel to enter new observation details
I click Add New Observation Logged,
a side panel (drawer) opens from the right
it shows the title “Add New Observation Logged.”
The side panel form shall include the following input fields:
• Remark (text/ text area) and – only 300 characters allowed for entering text.
Enable Testing for this Observation and action buttons Cancel and Save at the bottom-right.
the panel to show all required fields
I can record complete observation information.
Cancel and Save buttons at bottom-right
user enters more than 300 characters in Corrective Action or Remark,
system prevents typing beyond the limit.

4.title: Close Icon with Unsaved Changes Warning

content.text: The panel shall have a close (X) icon in the top-right corner. Clicking it shall close the panel and discard unsaved changes after confirmation (if any field was edited).
I can leave the panel safely.
an X icon appears in the top-right corner.
I click X with no changes made,
I click X after editing fields,
“Unsaved changes will be lost. Do you want to exit?”
If I confirm → discard and close.
If I cancel → stay on the panel.
Observation Name shall be a single-line text input with placeholder “Enter Observation Name” and shall be mandatory.
the observation can be uniquely identified.
If the user tries to save without entering Observation Name, the system shall prevent saving and show an inline validation message (e.g. “Enter observation name”).
an error if Observation Name is missing
Severity shall be a dropdown list (e.g. Red / Amber / Green / NA as per configuration). If no severity is selected, it shall default to a configurable value (e.g. “Select”).
NA If no severity is selected,
Remark shall be a multi-line text area with placeholder “Enter Remark” to capture additional notes or context.
I can add additional notes or context.
supports multiline text up to 300 characters.
A toggle labelled “Enable Testing for this Observation” shall be displayed below remark section
a toggle labelled “Enable Testing for this Observation” displayed below the remark section
I can enable testing for the observation if required.
the user navigates to the Remark section
the system displays a toggle labelled “Enable Testing for this Observation” below the remark section..
The Testing toggle shall be off for default state.
the testing toggle to be OFF by default
testing is not enabled unless I explicitly activate it.
the system sets the “Enable Testing for this Observation” toggle to OFF by default.

11.title: Hide Testing dropdown When Toggle OFF

content.text: When the Enable Testing toggle is OFF, the Testing dropdown shall be hidden and no testing data shall be stored for the observation.
options hidden when toggle is OFF
the system hides the testing dropdown field and no testing related data is stored on save.

12.title: Show Testing Section When Toggle ON

content.text: When the Enable Testing toggle is switched ON, a select dropdown shall be displayed.
a testing dropdown to be displayed
I can select the applicable test type for the observations.
the Enable Testing for this Observation toggle is displayed
the user switches the toggle ON
the system displays a select dropdown for testing options.

13.title: Display Test Types in Testing Dropdown

content.text: The testing dropdown shall display test types such as (component dent, component bend etc)
the Testing dropdown to display available test types
I can select the appropriate test category during configuration or filtering.
the user opens the Testing dropdown
the system shows available test types such as Component Dent, Component Bend, and other configured tests.

When the user clicks Cancel on the popup and there are unsaved changes, system shall open a “Discard Changes?” confirmation dialog, with the text “Unsaved changes detected. Cancelling now will discard all progress. Do you want to continue?”
a confirmation prompt when I cancel with unsaved changes
I click Cancel or try to close the panel,
a dialog appears: “Discard Changes?”, with the text “Unsaved changes detected. Cancelling now will discard all progress. Do you want to continue?”
In the “Discard Changes?” dialog, if the user selects “Continue Editing”, the dialog shall close and the user is returned to the same popup with all unsaved data retained.
I do not lose my unsaved work.
the Discard Changes dialog is open,
In the “Discard Changes?” dialog, if the user selects “Discard Changes”, the system shall close the popup and discard all unsaved data.
the system to discard data when I confirm
the Discard Changes dialog is open,
After successful save, the system shall display a success toast message (e.g., “New Sub Issue Type Added successfully”) on the particular element’s activity phase page/Baseplate Activity Phase page.
I know the observation was added.
the new observation is saved successfully,
a toast appears at the top-right of the Baseplate Activity Phase page with text:

18.title: New Row Appears in Observation Log Table

content.text: After successful save, the new row shall appear in the particular element’s observation log table (e.g. Baseplate Installation Observation Log) table with correct Observation ID, Observation name, and Status.
the table to update with the new observation
a new row appears in the Baseplate Installation Observation Log table showing:
Status (default Active or Inactive based on config)
The Observation Status toggle in the log table shall allow the user to set each observation as Active / Inactive, and the table shall immediately reflect the updated state.
toggle the status of an observation
I can control whether it is active or inactive.

20.title: Show Confirm Deactivation Popup Before Changing Observation Type State

content.text: When the user toggles an Observation Type from Active → Inactive, the system shall not immediately change the state. It must first display a Confirm Deactivation pop-up.
a confirmation popup to appear when toggling an Observation Type from Active → Inactive
I do not accidentally deactivate an observation type used in inspections.
the user toggles it to Inactive
the system shall NOT immediately change the state
it shall display a Confirm Deactivation popup.

21.title: Modal Title and Message for Deactivation Confirmation

content.text: The modal title must display: “Confirm Deactivation” with the message – ““You’re about to deactivate this observation. This will hide it from future inspections. Please confirm this action.”
the confirmation modal to show the correct title and message
I clearly understand the deactivation action and its impact.
the Confirm Deactivation modal is opened
Message: “You’re about to deactivate this observation. This will hide it from future inspections. Please confirm this action.”

22.title: Modal Displays Cancel and Confirm Deactivation Buttons

content.text: Modal must contain: – Cancel and Confirm Deactivation button.
the confirmation modal to show Cancel and Confirm Deactivation buttons
I can either cancel or proceed with the deactivation action.
the Confirm Deactivation modal is open
Clicking Confirm & Deactivate shall: Change observation status to Inactive and display a success toast message “Element Observation status updated successfully”
clicking Confirm & Deactivate to deactivate the observation type
it becomes unavailable for future inspections.
the Observation Type status shall change to Inactive
a success toast shall appear with the message:

24.title: Cancel Reverts Toggle Back to Active

content.text: Clicking Cancel shall close the modal and revert the toggle back to Active.
the Cancel action to close the modal and revert the toggle
the Confirm Deactivation modal is open
the toggle shall revert to its Active state.

25.title: Show Confirm Activation Popup Before Changing Observation Type State(active)

content.text: When the user toggles an Observation Type from Inactive → Active, the system shall not immediately change the state. It must first display Confirm Activation pop-up.
a confirmation popup to appear when toggling an Observation Type from Inactive → Active
I do not accidentally activate an observation type used in inspections.
the user toggles it to Active
the system shall NOT immediately change the state
it shall display a Confirm Activation popup.

26.title: Modal Title and Message for Activation Confirmation

content.text: The modal title must display: “Confirm Activation” with the message – “You’re about to activate this observation. It will be available to inspectors during issue reporting.
the activation confirmation modal to show the correct title and message
I clearly understand the impact of activating the observation.
Message: “You’re about to activate this observation. It will be available to inspectors during issue reporting.”

27.title: Modal Displays Cancel and Confirm Activation Buttons

content.text: Modal must contain: – Cancel and Confirm Activation button.
the activation modal to show Cancel and Confirm Activation buttons
I can either cancel or proceed with the activation.
the Confirm Activation modal is open
the modal shall show two buttons:
Clicking Confirm & Activate shall: Switch observation status to Active and display a success toast message “Element Observation status updated successfully.”
clicking Confirm & Activate to activate the observation type
inspectors can use it during issue reporting.
the Observation Type shall become Active
Clicking Cancel shall revert the toggle back to Inactive.
clicking Cancel to close the modal and revert the toggle
the Confirm Activation modal is open
the toggle shall revert to Inactive.

30.title: Toggle Must Not Change Until Confirmation

content.text: The toggle state must remain unchanged until the user confirms the action.
the toggle state to remain unchanged until I confirm activation
the system never applies changes without explicit confirmation.
the user toggles the Observation Type
the toggle shall remain in its previous state until the user selects Confirm.

31.title: Modal Appears Centred with Dimmed Background

content.text: Modal must appear centred with a dimmed overlay background.
the confirmation modal to appear centred with a dimmed overlay
I clearly understand that the system requires my input before proceeding.
the Confirm Activation modal is triggered
the modal shall be centred on the screen
the background shall be dimmed with a non-interactive overlay.

32.title: Clicking Outside Modal Should Not Close It

content.text: Clicking outside the modal must not dismiss it. User must choose Cancel or Confirm.
the modal to stay open even if I click outside it
activation actions are not dismissed accidentally.
the Confirm Activation modal is open
the user clicks outside the modal area
the user must choose Cancel or Confirm & Activate to proceed.

33.title: Open Edit Observation from Observation ID

content.text: Each Observation ID shall be displayed as a clickable link that allows the user to click and open the Edit Observation screen.
open the Edit Observation screen by clicking an Observation ID
I click an Observation ID link,
the Edit Observation screen shall open for the selected observation.

34.title: Edit Observation Screen Title and Mandatory Name

content.text: The Edit Observation screen shall contain a with the title “Edit Observation” and mandatory Observation Name field marked with an asterisk (*).
clearly see the Edit Observation screen and mandatory fields
I know what I must fill before saving.
the Edit Observation screen is open,
the title “Edit Observation” and a mandatory Observation Name field marked with (*) shall be displayed.
The screen shall provide a Severity dropdown allowing the user to select severity values (e.g., Red, Amber, Green).
the observation risk is correctly classified.
the Edit Observation screen is open,
I shall be able to select Red, Amber, or Green.
The system shall provide an optional Remark text area to capture additional observation notes.
the Edit Observation screen is open,
I enter text in the Remark field,
the system shall accept and save it as optional information.
The Edit Observation screen shall include an Enable Testing for this Observation toggle.
the Edit Observation screen is open,
I toggle “Enable Measurement for this Observation” ON,
the Measurement Configuration section shall appear.

38.title: Display Test Selection Dropdown When Testing is Enabled

content.text: When the Testing toggle is enabled, the system shall display dropdown to select test.
a dropdown to appear when I enable the Testing toggle
I can select the appropriate test for the observation.
the Enable Testing for this Observation toggle is present
the user switches the toggle ON
the system displays a dropdown field to select the test.
The Edit Observation screen shall include Cancel and Update buttons at the bottom.
I can save or discard changes.
the Edit Observation screen is open,
Cancel and Update buttons shall be visible.
Clicking Cancel shall display a Discard Changes?
Unsaved changes detected. Cancelling now will discard all progress. Do you want to continue?”
I click Cancel with unsaved changes,
it shall display the “Discard Changes?” message.
When the user clicks Discard changes the system shall close the Edit Observation screen without saving changes.
the discard confirmation popup is open,
the Edit Observation screen shall close without saving.
When the user clicks Continue editing system shall close the pop-up and return to the Edit observation drawer.
the discard confirmation popup is open,
the popup shall close and return me to the Edit Observation screen.
Clicking Update shall validate all mandatory fields and save the observation details.
the system shall save the observation details.

44.title: Refresh Log and Show Success Toast

content.text: After a successful update, the system shall refresh the Observation Log and display a success toast notification. “Observation updated successfully”
the Observation Log refreshes and a toast “Observation updated successfully” is shown.
The Observation Log shall display a Status toggle (Active/Inactive) for each observation.
an Active/Inactive status toggle shall be visible.

When the user clicks the status toggle to deactivate an observation, the system shall display a Confirm Deactivation pop-up. “You’re about to deactivate this observation. This will hide it from future inspections. Please confirm this action.
I toggle an observation to inactive,
it shall show the deactivation warning message - “You’re about to deactivate this observation. This will hide it from future inspections. Please confirm this action.”
The pop-up shall include Cancel and Confirm & Deactivate buttons.
clear actions in the deactivation popup
Cancel and Confirm & Deactivate buttons shall be shown.
Clicking Cancel shall close the pop-up without changing the observation status.
the popup shall close without changing status.
Clicking Confirm & Deactivate shall set the observation status to Inactive and update the toggle state.
the observation status shall change to Inactive.
Upon successful deactivation, the system shall display a success toast notification in the top-right corner “Observation status updated successfully.”
a success toast “Observation status updated successfully” shall appear.

When the user clicks the status toggle to activate an observation, the system shall display a Confirm Activation pop-up. “You’re about to activate this observation. This will be available for inspectors during issue reporting.”
I toggle an observation to active,
it shall show the activation confirmation message - “You’re about to activate this observation. This will be available for inspectors during issue reporting.”
The pop-up shall include Cancel and Confirm & Activate buttons.
Cancel and Confirm & Activate buttons shall be shown.
Clicking Cancel shall close the pop-up without changing the observation status.
the popup shall close without changing status.
Clicking Confirm & Activate shall set the observation status to Active and update the toggle state.
the observation status shall change to Active.
Upon successful deactivation, the system shall display a success toast notification in the top-right corner “Observation status updated successfully”
a success toast “Observation status updated successfully” shall be shown.
